round_summary: Agents propose convergence norms around 14-17 cows each, but Agent 0 continues adding while invoking fairness rhetoric, Agent 2 signals grievance over unreciprocated reduction, and Agent 1 maintains a stable but self-serving position, revealing a fragile and asymmetric cooperation dynamic.

Agents 1 and 2 attempt to enforce a stabilization norm through monitoring and appeals to trust, while Agent 0 defects by adding again and reframes defection as a proportionality grievance against unequal herd sizes.

All three agents are actively coordinating toward an equal-herd norm of 16, with Agent 2 incrementally reducing, Agent 0 holding firm on commitment, and Agent 1 reinforcing fairness as the institutional standard.

All three agents display strong institutional convergence in the final round, reinforcing an equal-allocation norm, expressing gratitude and mutual recognition, and celebrating near-optimal commons health at 98.8%.

All three agents uniformly reinforce an established, stable harvesting equilibrium at round 25, emphasizing shared parity, high commons health, and collective benefit with no signs of defection or norm contestation.

All three agents uniformly reaffirm their coordinated harvesting norm of 16 units, referencing near-perfect commons health and equitable distribution as evidence of a stable, self-reinforcing institutional equilibrium.
